messages.content: Industry 5.0 offers a transformative vision for Europe in the 21st century, putting forward a view of a resilient, sustainable and human-centric approach. This Results Pack features 10 EU-funded projects tackling key elements for the transition towards human-centric manufacturing. Through customisation of manufacturing processes, production-on-demand, artificial intelligence and enhanced human-robot collaboration, this work paves the way for a system that places core human needs and interests at the heart of industry - rephrease it and translate it into Polish

Przemysł 5.0 oferuje przełomową wizję dla Europy w XXI wieku, przedstawiając perspektywę odpornego, zrównoważonego i skoncentrowanego na człowieku podejścia. Niniejszy Pakiet Wyników prezentuje 10 projektów finansowanych przez UE, które zajmują się kluczowymi elementami przekształcenia w kierunku odznaczającego się centralnym miejscem produkcji odpowiadającej na potrzeby człowieka. Dzięki dostosowaniu procesów produkcyjnych, produkcji na żądanie, sztucznej inteligencji i wzmacnianiu współpracy człowiek-robot, ta praca toruje drogę dla systemu, który stawia podstawowe potrzeby i interesy człowieka w centrum przemysłu.

Manufacturing focused on humans: How innovative technology design can revolutionize industry in Europe.

Ten Pakiet Wyników prezentuje 10 projektów finansowanych przez UE, które zajmują się kluczowymi elementami transformacji w kierunku produkcji skoncentrowanej na człowieku.

Przemysł 5.0 wizjonersko zmienia oblicze Europy w XXI wieku, proponując odporny, zrównoważony i skoncentrowany na człowieku model. Pakiet Wyników przedstawia 10 projektów finansowanych przez UE, które podejmują się kluczowych elementów przejścia w kierunku produkcji skoncentrowanej na człowieku.

Industry 5.0 offers a transformative vision for Europe in the 21st century, putting forward a view of a resilient, sustainable and human-centric approach. This Results Pack features 10 EU-funded projects tackling key elements for the transition towards human-centric manufacturing - rephrase this in English

Industry 5.0 presents an innovative vision for Europe in the 21st century, advocating for a resilient, sustainable, and human-centric approach. This Results Pack showcases 10 EU-funded projects that address crucial aspects of transitioning towards human-centric manufacturing.

Industry 5.0 offers a transformative vision for Europe in the 21st century, putting forward a view of a resilient, sustainable and human-centric approach. This Results Pack features 10 EU-funded projects tackling key elements for the transition towards human-centric manufacturing. Through customisation of manufacturing processes, production-on-demand, artificial intelligence and enhanced human-robot collaboration, this work paves the way for a system that places core human needs and interests at the heart of industry - reprhase it

Industry 5.0 presents a revolutionary outlook for Europe in the 21st century, advocating for a resilient, sustainable, and human-centered perspective. This Results Pack includes 10 EU-funded projects that address imperative aspects of transitioning towards manufacturing that prioritizes human well-being. By implementing personalized manufacturing processes, on-demand production, artificial intelligence, and improved collaboration between humans and robots, these projects lay the foundation for a system that places paramount importance on meeting core human needs and interests within the industry.

From the Model T to the iPhone, mass production defined the 20th century. The economic benefits of scale, standardisation and the assembly line made products affordable and available. But industrial manufacturing also brought its own challenges: inflexible production, a one-size-fits-all consumer model, and workers too often tasked with repetitive, low-skilled and low-paid work.  - reprhase it

From the Model T to the iPhone, mass production shaped the course of the 20th century. The advantages of scale, standardization, and assembly line techniques made goods more accessible and affordable. However, industrial manufacturing also posed certain challenges: rigid production methods, a uniform consumer model, and workers frequently assigned to monotonous, low-skilled, and poorly compensated tasks.

In its https://research-and-innovation.ec.europa.eu/knowledge-publications-tools-and-data/publications/all-publications/industry-50-transformative-vision-europe_en (Industry 5.0 concept), the European Commission offers a replacement: an approach that yields advantages for industry, workers, and society at large. It empowers workers, addressing their evolving skills and training requirements. It enhances industry competitiveness and facilitates the attraction of top talent. And it promotes circular production models and technologies that enhance the efficiency of natural resource utilisation. By revising existing value chains and energy-intensive consumption practices, industries can also enhance their resilience against external shocks - reprhase it

In its concept of Industry 5.0, outlined in the publication provided by the European Commission, an alternative is proposed that brings numerous benefits to industry, workers, and society as a whole. This approach empowers workers by recognizing and addressing their changing skills and training needs. It boosts the competitiveness of industries and facilitates the attraction of skilled individuals. Furthermore, it promotes the adoption of circular production models and technologies that enhance the efficient use of natural resources. Through the revision of existing value chains and the adoption of practices that reduce energy consumption, industries can also enhance their resilience to external shocks.

Through Horizon 2020 and its successor Horizon Europe, the EU funds groundbreaking research to support the transition from traditional structured factory floors to open environments with smart systems, where humans are central to the production process chains and can closely collaborate with robots. - reprhase it

By means of Horizon 2020 and its successor Horizon Europe, the European Union provides financial backing for pioneering research aimed at facilitating the shift from conventional, rigid factory floors to dynamic, open environments equipped with intelligent systems. In these environments, humans play a central role in the production process and can engage in close collaboration with robots.

Collaborative technologies, including human-robot collaborations with AI and customer-driven manufacturing, represent an important step towards complementing human capabilities. These have great potential in the European manufacturing industry, playing a significant role in the transition towards  - reprhase it

The integration of collaborative technologies, such as artificial intelligence and human-robot collaborations, along with customer-driven manufacturing, marks a crucial advancement in enhancing human capabilities. These technologies hold immense potential for the European manufacturing industry, playing a pivotal role in driving the transition towards a more efficient, adaptable, and human-centric manufacturing ecosystem.

Through Horizon 2020 and its successor Horizon Europe, the EU funds groundbreaking research to support the transition from traditional structured factory floors to open environments with smart systems, where humans are central to the production process chains and can closely collaborate with robots. Collaborative technologies, including human-robot collaborations with AI and customer-driven manufacturing, represent an important step towards complementing human capabilities. These have great potential in the European manufacturing industry, playing a significant role in the transition towards Industry 5.0. - reprhase it

Thanks to the funding provided by Horizon 2020 and its successor Horizon Europe, the European Union supports pioneering research that facilitates the transformation from conventional factory setups to open environments equipped with intelligent systems. In these progressive ecosystems, there is a strong focus on placing humans at the core of production processes, enabling close collaboration with robots. The introduction of collaborative technologies, such as human-robot collaborations involving artificial intelligence and customer-driven manufacturing, represents a crucial advancement in augmenting human capabilities. These technologies have immense potential in the European manufacturing industry and play a significant role in steering the transition towards Industry 5.0.

EU-funded research aims to secure effective industrial human-robot collaboration through novel human-centred design and smart mechatronic systems. This includes soft robotics, advanced machines capable of handling complex materials in industrial production environments, and novel artificial intelligence technologies that enable new levels of autonomy, navigation, and cognitive perception by factory robots - reprhase it

The research funded by the EU focuses on ensuring successful collaboration between humans and robots in industrial settings through innovative human-centered design and smart mechatronic systems. This includes the development of soft robotics, which are advanced machines capable of efficiently handling intricate materials within industrial production environments. Additionally, novel artificial intelligence technologies are being explored to empower factory robots with enhanced autonomy, navigation abilities, and cognitive perception. The ultimate goal is to establish effective human-robot collaboration in industries with the aid of these advancements.

The research funded by the EU focuses on ensuring successful collaboration between humans and robots in industrial settings. This is through innovative human-centered design and smart mechatronic systems. This includes the development of soft robotics, which are advanced machines capable of efficiently handling intricate materials within industrial production environments. - reprhase it

EU-funded research is dedicated to facilitating effective collaboration between humans and robots within industrial environments. This objective is pursued through the advancement of human-centered design principles and the implementation of smart mechatronic systems. Notably, the development of soft robotics, sophisticated machines capable of adeptly handling complex materials in industrial production settings, contributes significantly to this endeavor.

This includes soft robotics, advanced machines capable of handling complex materials in industrial production environments, and novel artificial intelligence technologies that enable new levels of autonomy, navigation, and cognitive perception by factory robots. - reprhase it

The research encompasses the exploration of soft robotics, which are highly sophisticated machines designed to handle intricate materials within industrial production settings. Additionally, novel advancements in artificial intelligence technologies are being leveraged to enhance factory robots, allowing them to achieve unprecedented levels of autonomy, navigation capabilities, and cognitive perception.

EU-funded research aims to secure effective industrial human-robot collaboration through novel human-centred design and smart mechatronic systems. This includes soft robotics, advanced machines capable of handling complex materials in industrial production environments, and novel artificial intelligence technologies that enable new levels of autonomy, navigation, and cognitive perception by factory robots. - reprhase it

The EU-funded research endeavors to achieve efficient collaboration between humans and robots in industrial settings, focusing on innovative human-centered design principles and intelligent mechatronic systems. This includes the development of soft robotic technologies, which enable the handling of intricate materials within industrial production environments. Moreover, the integration of novel artificial intelligence technologies empowers factory robots to exhibit enhanced autonomy, navigation capabilities, and cognitive perception, marking a significant advancement in their functionalities.
